title: Fresh Floral Kenyan Cucumber Salad
description: A light, kid-friendly salad bursting with fresh cucumber, pickled crunch, and a floral touch, made with simple lowFODMAP ingredients for a quick Kenyan-inspired lunch.
instructions: Wash the cucumbers thoroughly and slice them into thin rounds or half‑moons, discarding the skin if you prefer.
Place the sliced cucumbers in a large mixing bowl and sprinkle with the chopped mint leaves.
Add the dried edible flowers and the pickled cucumber slices to the bowl.
In a small bowl, whisk together the vegetable oil, lemon juice, honey (if using), salt, and pepper until the dressing is emulsified.
Pour the dressing over the cucumber mixture, tossing gently to coat all ingredients evenly.
If desired, sprinkle chia seeds on top for a slight crunch and stir lightly.
Taste and adjust seasoning, adding more salt or lemon juice if needed.
Transfer the salad to a serving dish or individual bowls, ensuring each child gets a generous portion.
Serve immediately, or cover and refrigerate for up to 2 hours to let flavors meld.
Encourage kids to pick up the salad with small spoons or forks and enjoy the fresh, floral crunch.
